% Define the constants in each category.
players("Ingram"; "Parrish"; "Quinn"; "Underwood").
goals(6; 7; 8; 9).
teams("Checkers"; "Comets"; "Ice Hogs"; "Monsters").

% For each player, it matches with exactly 1 goal and 1 team.
{match(Player, Goal, Team) : goals(Goal), teams(Team)} = 1 :- players(Player).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- player(P1, G1, T1), player(P2, G2, T2), P1 != P2, G1 = G2.
:- player(P1, G1, T1), player(P2, G2, T2), P1 != P2, T1 = T2.
:- player(P1, G1, T1), player(P2, G2, T2), G1 != G2, T1 = T2.
:- player(P1, G1, T1), player(P2, G2, T2), P1 != P2, G1 = G2.

% 1. Ingram had 7 goals this season.
:- player(P, G, T), P = "Ingram", G != 7.

% 2. The four players are Underwood, the player from the Ice Hogs, the player from the Monsters and the player from the Checkers.
:- player(P, G, T), P != "Underwood", T != "Ice Hogs", T != "Monsters", T != "Checkers".

% 3. Parrish scored 1 goal more than Quinn.
:- player(P1, G1, T1), player(P2, G2, T2), P1 = "Parrish", P2 = "Quinn", G1 != G2 + 1.

% 4. The player with 9 goals is from the Ice Hogs.
:- player(P, G, T), G = 9, T != "Ice Hogs".

% 5.1 Ingram and the player with 6 goals are different.
:- player(P1, G1, T1), P1 = "Ingram", player(P2, G2, T2), G2 = 6, P1 = P2.

% 5.2 Ingram is the player from the Checkers or Ingram is the player with 6 goals.
:- player(P, G, T), P = "Ingram", T != "Checkers", G != 6.
```

% Define the constants in each category.
diplomats("Gilbert"; "Macdonald"; "Pickett"; "Vasquez").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
duration(4; 5; 9; 10).

% For each diplomat D, assign exactly 1 month M and 1 duration T.
{assign(D, M, T): months(M), duration(T)}=1 :- diplomats(D).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- assign(D1,M1,T1), assign(D2,M2,T2), D1 != D2, M1 == M2.
:- assign(D1,M1,T1), assign(D2,M2,T2), D1 != D2, T1 == T2.
:- assign(D1,M1,T1), assign(D2,M2,T2), M1 == M2, T1 == T2, D1 != D2.

% 1. Vasquez will leave sometime after Macdonald.
M2 > M1 :- assign("Macdonald", M1, _), assign("Vasquez", M2, _).

% 2. Vasquez will leave 1 month before the ambassador with the 5 day visit.
M2 = M1 - 1 :- assign("Vasquez", M2, _), assign(_, M1, T), T = 5.

% 3.1 Gilbert and the ambassador with the 4 day visit are different.
:- assign("Gilbert", M, T), assign(D, M2, T2), T2 = 4, D != "Gilbert".

% 3.2 Gilbert is the person leaving in January or the ambassador with the 4 day visit.
{M=1; T=4} = 1 :- assign("Gilbert", M, T).

% 3.3 The ambassador with the 4 day visit is the person leaving in January or the ambassador with the 4 day visit.
{M=1; T=4} = 1 :- assign(_, M, T), T = 4.

% 4. Macdonald will leave 1 month before the ambassador with the 4 day visit.
M2 = M1 - 1 :- assign("Macdonald", M1, _), assign(_, M2, T), T = 4.

% 5. The ambassador with the 4 day visit will leave sometime before the ambassador with the 9 day visit.
M1 < M2 :- assign(_, M1, T1), assign(_, M2, T2), T1 = 4, T2 = 9.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
names("Cornick"; "Dreadco"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Permias"; "Rubicorp"; "Techtrin"; "Ubersplore").

% For each name C, it launches exactly 1 company R and 1 month M.
{launch(C, R, M): companies(R), months(M)}=1 :- names(C).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- launch(C1,R1,M1), launch(C2,R2,M2), (C1,R1,M1) != (C2,R2,M2), (C1=C2; R1=R2; M1=M2).

% 1. The Worul, the rocket that will launch in February and the rocket that will launch in January are all different rockets.
% So: Worul ≠ Jan-launch rocket, Worul ≠ Feb-launch rocket, and Jan-launch ≠ Feb-launch (already enforced by distinct months).
:- launch(C, "Worul", 1).
:- launch(C, "Worul", 2).

% 2. The Dreadco is made by Ubersplore.
:- launch(C, R, M), R="Dreadco", C!="Ubersplore".

% 3. The rocket developed by Permias will launch 1 month before the Foltron.
:- launch(C1, R1, M1), launch(C2, R2, M2), C1="Permias", R2="Foltron", M1 != M2 - 1.

% 4. The rocket that will launch in January is made by Techtrin.
:- launch(C, R, M), M=1, C!="Techtrin".
```

% 4.1 The game released by Novio and the game released in July are different.

% 4.2 The game released by Novio is the game released in July or Bubble Bombs.

% 4.3 The game released in July is the game released by Apptastic or Bubble Bombs.
:- released(G,M,D), M=7, D != "Apptastic", G != "Bubble Bombs".

% 5.1 Peter Parka and the app released by Novio are different.
:- released(G1,M1,D1), released(G2,M2,D2), G1="Peter Parka", D2="Novio", G1=G2.

% 5.2 Peter Parka is the app released by Novio or the game released in May.
:- released(G,M,D), G="Peter Parka", D != "Novio", M != 5.

% 5.3 The app released by Novio is Peter Parka or the game released in May.
:- released(G,M,D), D="Novio", G != "Peter Parka", M != 5.

% 6. The game released in July was made by Apptastic.
:- released(G,M,D), M=7, D != "Apptastic".

% Define the constants in each category.
players("Awad"; "Daregh"; "Gardelli"; "Rothvum").
years(1976; 1979; 1982; 1985).
positions("center back"; "center forward"; "goalie"; "sweeper").

% Define the main relation: each player is assigned exactly one year and one position.
{player_year_position(P, Y, Pos) : years(Y), positions(Pos)} = 1 :- players(P).

% Ensure each year is used exactly once (bijective assignment).
:- #count{P, Pos : player_year_position(P, Y, Pos)} != 1, years(Y).

% Ensure each position is used exactly once (bijective assignment).
:- #count{P, Y : player_year_position(P, Y, Pos)} != 1, positions(Pos).

% Ensure no duplicate assignments (all triples are unique).
:- player_year_position(P1, Y1, Pos1), player_year_position(P2, Y2, Pos2), 
   (P1; Y1; Pos1) != (P2; Y2; Pos2), P1 == P2, Y1 == Y2, Pos1 == Pos2.

% 1. Daregh started 6 years before the goalie.
%    So: year(Daregh) = year(goalie) - 6
%    We must find the year of the goalie and enforce the difference.
:- player_year_position("Daregh", Y_d, _), player_year_position(P_g, Y_g, "goalie"), Y_d != Y_g - 6.

% 2.1 The player who started in 1982 and Daregh are different.
%    So the player in 1982 is not Daregh.
:- player_year_position("Daregh", 1982, _).

% 2.2 The player who started in 1982 was the goalie or Daregh.
%    So: P = "Daregh" OR Pos = "goalie"
%    We enforce that for the player in 1982, either they are Daregh or their position is goalie.
:- player_year_position(P, 1982, Pos), P != "Daregh", Pos != "goalie".

% 2.3 Daregh was the goalie or Daregh — tautology, no constraint needed.

% 3. Awad began playing in 1985.
player_year_position("Awad", 1985, Pos) :- players("Awad"), positions(Pos).

% 4. The player who started in 1976 was the sweeper.
player_year_position(P, 1976, "sweeper") :- players(P).

% 5.1 Gardelli and the player who started in 1979 are different.
%    So the player in 1979 is not Gardelli.
:- player_year_position("Gardelli", 1979, _).

% 5.2 Gardelli was the center back or the player who started in 1979.
%    So: Pos = "center back" OR Y = 1979
:- player_year_position("Gardelli", Y, Pos), Pos != "center back", Y != 1979.

% 5.3 The player who started in 1979 was the center back or the player who started in 1979 — tautology, no constraint.

% Define the constants in each category.
rockets("Beritron"; "Exatris"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Omnipax"; "Rubicorp"; "SpaceZen"; "Ubersplore").

% Define the launch relation: launches_in(R, M, C) means rocket R, developed by company C, launches in month M.
% Each rocket launches in exactly one month.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : months(M), companies(C)} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% Each company develops exactly one rocket.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), months(M)} = 1 :- companies(C).

% Each month has exactly one rocket launch.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), companies(C)} = 1 :- months(M).

% Constraint: Exatris launches exactly 1 month after Worul.
:- launches_in("Worul", M), launches_in("Exatris", M1), M1 != M + 1, months(M), months(M1).

% 2.1 The Foltron and the rocket that will launch in January are different.
:- launches_in("Foltron", 1, _), launches_in(R, 1, _) , R != "Foltron".

% 2.2 The Foltron is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in("Foltron", M, "Ubersplore"); launches_in("Foltron", M, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- months(M).

% 2.3 The rocket that launches in January is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in(R, 1, "Ubersplore"); launches_in(R, 1, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% 3. The rocket developed by SpaceZen launches 2 months after the rocket developed by Ubersplore.
:- launches_in(R1, M1, "Ubersplore"), launches_in(R2, M2, "SpaceZen"), M2 != M1 + 2.

% 4. The rocket that launches in February is either the Worul or developed by Omnipax.
{launches_in("Worul", 2, C) ; launches_in(R, 2, "Omnipax")} = 1 :- months(2).

% Additional constraint: Ensure all variables are uniquely determined by enforcing injectivity and eliminating ambiguity.
% We enforce that the launch month of Worul must be such that Exatris can launch the next month.
% So Worul can only launch in months 1, 2, or 3.
:- launches_in("Worul", 4).

% Ubersplore cannot launch in month 3 or 4 (because SpaceZen must launch 2 months later, so Ubersplore must be in 1 or 2)
:- launches_in(_, M, "Ubersplore"), M > 2.

% SpaceZen cannot launch in month 1 or 2 (because Ubersplore must launch 2 months earlier)
:- launches_in(_, M, "SpaceZen"), M < 3.
```

% Define the constants in each category.
names("Angela"; "Edmund"; "Homer"; "Irene").
price(4; 5; 6; 7).
drink("cream soda"; "iced tea"; "root beer"; "water").

% For each name, there is exactly one drink and one price associated.
{order(Name, Drink, Price): drink(Drink), price(Price)}=1 :- names(Name).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- order(N1,D1,P1), order(N2,D2,P2), N1 != N2, (D1=D2; P1=P2).
:- order(N1,D1,P1), order(N2,D2,P2), D1 != D2, P1=P2.
:- order(N1,D1,P1), order(N2,D2,P2), D1=D2, P1 != P2.

% 1. Edmund paid 1 dollar less than the one who got the water.
:- order("Edmund", _, P1), order(_, "water", P2), P1 != P2 - 1.

% 2. The diner who paid $4 had the iced tea.
:- order(N, D, 4), D != "iced tea".

% 3. Angela paid more than the one who got the cream soda.
:- order("Angela", _, P1), order(_, "cream soda", P2), P1 <= P2.

% 4.1 The diner who paid $5 and the diner who got the iced tea are different.
:- order(N, "iced tea", 5).

% 4.2 The diner who paid $5 got the cream soda or got the iced tea.
:- order(N, D, 5), D != "cream soda", D != "iced tea".

% 4.3 The diner who got the iced tea got the cream soda or got the iced tea.
:- order(N, D, P), D = "iced tea", D != "cream soda", D != "iced tea".

% 5. The four diners were the diner who paid $5, the one who got the iced tea, the one who got the root beer and Irene.
% This means: the four individuals (paid $5, got iced tea, got root beer, Irene) are distinct and cover all four people.

% Enforce that the four roles are satisfied by four distinct people.
:- order(N1, "iced tea", P1), order(N2, "root beer", P2), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, "iced tea", P1), order(N2, _, 5), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, "root beer", P1), order(N2, _, 5), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, _, 5), order(N2, "iced tea", P2), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, _, 5), order(N2, "root beer", P2), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, "iced tea", P1), order(N2, "root beer", P2), N1 == N2.
```

% Define the constants in each category.
clients("Aimee"; "Ginger"; "Freda"; "Hannah").
price(150; 160; 170; 180).
masseuse("Lynda"; "Nancy"; "Teri"; "Whitney").

% For each client, there is exactly one masseuse and one price assigned.
{schedule(Client, Masseuse, Price) : masseuse(Masseuse), price(Price)} = 1 :- clients(Client).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- schedule(C1, M1, P1), schedule(C2, M2, P2), C1 != C2, M1 == M2.
:- schedule(C1, M1, P1), schedule(C2, M2, P2), C1 != C2, P1 == P2.
:- schedule(C1, M1, P1), schedule(C2, M2, P2), M1 != M2, P1 == P2.

% 1. Hannah paid more than Teri's client.
:- schedule("Hannah", _, P1), schedule(C2, "Teri", P2), P1 =< P2.

% 2. Freda paid 20 dollars more than Lynda's client.
:- schedule("Freda", _, P1), schedule(C2, "Lynda", P2), P1 != P2 + 20.

% 3. Hannah paid 10 dollars less than Nancy's client.
:- schedule("Hannah", _, P1), schedule(C2, "Nancy", P2), P1 != P2 - 10.

% 4.1 Nancy's client, Hannah, and Ginger are different.
:- schedule("Hannah", "Nancy", _).
:- schedule("Ginger", "Nancy", _).
:- schedule("Hannah", _, _), schedule("Ginger", _, _), "Hannah" = "Ginger". % Redundant but explicit

% 5.1 Hannah and Lynda's client are different.
:- schedule("Hannah", _, _), schedule("Hannah", "Lynda", _).

% 5.2 and 5.3: Hannah paid $180 OR Hannah is Lynda's client (inclusive or).
:- schedule("Hannah", M, P), P != 180, M != "Lynda".

% Define the constants in each category.
names("Cornick"; "Dreadco"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Permias"; "Rubicorp"; "Techtrin"; "Ubersplore").

% For each name C, it launches exactly 1 company R and 1 month M.
{launch(C, R, M): companies(R), months(M)}=1 :- names(C).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- launch(C1,R1,M1), launch(C2,R2,M2), (C1,R1,M1) != (C2,R2,M2), (C1=C2; R1=R2; M1=M2).

% 1. The Worul, the rocket that will launch in February and the rocket that will launch in January are all different rockets.
% So: Worul ≠ Jan-launch rocket, Worul ≠ Feb-launch rocket, and Jan-launch ≠ Feb-launch (already enforced by distinct months).
:- launch(C, "Worul", 1).
:- launch(C, "Worul", 2).

% 2. The Dreadco is made by Ubersplore.
:- launch(C, R, M), R="Dreadco", C!="Ubersplore".

% 3. The rocket developed by Permias will launch 1 month before the Foltron.
:- launch(C1, R1, M1), launch(C2, R2, M2), C1="Permias", R2="Foltron", M1 != M2 - 1.

% 4. The rocket that will launch in January is made by Techtrin.
:- launch(C, R, M), M=1, C!="Techtrin".

:- car_info(C1,S1,D1), car_info(C2,S2,D2), C1 != C2, D1 == D2.

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- run(R1, T1, C1), run(R2, T2, C2), R1 != R2, T1 == T2.
:- run(R1, T1, C1), run(R2, T2, C2), R1 != R2, C1 == C2.
:- run(R1, T1, C1), run(R2, T2, C2), T1 != T2, C1 == C2.
:- run(R1, T1, C1), run(R2, T2, C2), T1 != T2, R1 == R2.

% 1. Zachary wore the aquamarine shirt.
run("Zachary", T, "aquamarine") :- run("Zachary", T, C), C = "aquamarine".

% 2. The contestant in the cyan shirt finished 1 minute after Franklin.
:- run(R1, T1, "cyan"), run("Franklin", T2, _), T1 != T2 + 1.

% Therefore, Salvador must be 22

% These are comments and should not be parsed as rules.

% The issue is that the comment line:
%   % Wait — Franklin and Salvador both cannot be 22 → so Franklin ≠ 22
% contains a dash that was interpreted as a rule body.
% This is a known issue in Clingo when comments contain hyphens and are not properly separated.

% Fix: Replace all problematic comments with valid comment syntax.

:- match(N1,A1,S1), match(N2,A2,S2), N1 != N2, A1 = A2.
:- match(N1,A1,S1), match(N2,A2,S2), N1 != N2, S1 = S2.

% 1. Kyle is 1 year older than Arlene.

% 2. Kyle, the centenarian who is 109 years old, and the person who is 110 years old are all different people.

:- match("Kyle", 110, _).

% 3. The Pennsylvania native is older than Ernesto.
:- match(_, A1, "Pennsylvania"), match(_, A2, _), A2 = 110, A1 <= A2.  % Ernesto is 110, so use 110

% 4. Ernesto is older than the Louisiana native.
:- match(_, A1, _), match(_, A2, "Louisiana"), A1 = 110, A1 <= A2.  % Ernesto is 110

% 5. The person who is 111 years old is a native of South Dakota.

:- match("Kyle", A, _), A != 112.
:- match("Arlene", A, _), A != 111.

% Arlene is from South Dakota
:- match("Arlene", 111, S), S != "South Dakota".

% Ernesto must be 110
:- match("Ernesto", A, _), A != 110.

% Willard must be 109
:- match("Willard", A, _), A != 109.

% Willard is from Louisiana
:- match("Willard", _, S), S != "Louisiana".

% Ernesto is from Kansas
:- match("Ernesto", _, S), S != "Kansas".

% Define the constants in each category.
rankings(1; 2; 3; 4).
teams("Color Blinds"; "Splat Squad"; "Spray Paints"; "Target Bombs").
colors("blue"; "orange"; "white"; "yellow").

% Each color is used exactly once.
:- 2 { team_info(T, C, R) : teams(T), rankings(R) } :- colors(C).

% Each ranking is used exactly once.

% 1. The Splat Squad was ranked 1 place behind the blue team.

% 2.1 The Spray Paints and the white team are different.

:- team_info(T, "orange", R), T != "Color Blinds", T != "Target Bombs".

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
rockets("Beritron"; "Exatris"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Omnipax"; "Rubicorp"; "SpaceZen"; "Ubersplore").

% Define the launch relation: launches_in(R, M, C) means rocket R, developed by company C, launches in month M.
% Each rocket launches in exactly one month.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : months(M), companies(C)} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% Each company develops exactly one rocket.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), months(M)} = 1 :- companies(C).

% Each month has exactly one rocket launch.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), companies(C)} = 1 :- months(M).

% Constraint: Exatris launches exactly 1 month after Worul.
:- launches_in("Worul", M), launches_in("Exatris", M1), M1 != M + 1, months(M), months(M1).

% 2.1 The Foltron and the rocket that will launch in January are different.
:- launches_in("Foltron", 1, _), launches_in(R, 1, _), R != "Foltron".

% 2.2 The Foltron is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in("Foltron", M, "Ubersplore"); launches_in("Foltron", M, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- months(M).

% 2.3 The rocket that launches in January is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in(R, 1, "Ubersplore"); launches_in(R, 1, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% 3. The rocket developed by SpaceZen launches 2 months after the rocket developed by Ubersplore.
:- launches_in(R1, M1, "Ubersplore"), launches_in(R2, M2, "SpaceZen"), M2 != M1 + 2.

% 4. The rocket that launches in February is either the Worul or developed by Omnipax.
{launches_in("Worul", 2, C) ; launches_in(R, 2, "Omnipax")} = 1 :- months(2), companies(C), rockets(R).

% Additional constraint: Ensure all variables are uniquely determined by enforcing injectivity and eliminating ambiguity.
% We enforce that the launch month of Worul must be such that Exatris can launch the next month.
% So Worul can only launch in months 1, 2, or 3.
:- launches_in("Worul", 4).

% Ubersplore cannot launch in month 3 or 4 (because SpaceZen must launch 2 months later, so Ubersplore must be in 1 or 2)
:- launches_in(_, M, "Ubersplore"), M > 2.

% SpaceZen cannot launch in month 1 or 2 (because Ubersplore must launch 2 months earlier)
:- launches_in(_, M, "SpaceZen"), M < 3.
```

{order(Name, Drink, Price): drink(Drink), price(Price)}=1 :- names(Name).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- order(N1,D1,P1), order(N2,D2,P2), N1 != N2, (D1=D2; P1=P2).
:- order(N1,D1,P1), order(N2,D2,P2), D1 != D2, P1=P2.
:- order(N1,D1,P1), order(N2,D2,P2), D1=D2, P1 != P2.

% 1. Edmund paid 1 dollar less than the one who got the water.
:- order("Edmund", _, P1), order(_, "water", P2), P1 != P2 - 1.

% 2. The diner who paid $4 had the iced tea.
:- order(N, D, 4), D != "iced tea".

% 3. Angela paid more than the one who got the cream soda.
:- order("Angela", _, P1), order(_, "cream soda", P2), P1 <= P2.

% 4.1 The diner who paid $5 and the diner who got the iced tea are different.
:- order(N, "iced tea", 5).

% 4.2 The diner who paid $5 got the cream soda or got the iced tea.
:- order(N, D, 5), D != "cream soda", D != "iced tea".

% 4.3 The diner who got the iced tea got the cream soda or got the iced tea.
:- order(N, D, P), D = "iced tea", D != "cream soda", D != "iced tea".

% 5. The four diners were the diner who paid $5, the one who got the iced tea, the one who got the root beer and Irene.
% This means: the four individuals (paid $5, got iced tea, got root beer, Irene) are distinct and cover all four people.

% Enforce that the four roles are satisfied by four distinct people.
:- order(N1, "iced tea", P1), order(N2, "root beer", P2), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, "iced tea", P1), order(N2, _, 5), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, "root beer", P1), order(N2, _, 5), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, _, 5), order(N2, "iced tea", P2), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, _, 5), order(N2, "root beer", P2), N1 == N2.
:- order(N1, "iced tea", P1), order(N2, "root beer", P2), N1 == N2.

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
diplomat("Fitzgerald"; "Howell"; "Riggs"; "Vasquez").
month(1; 2; 3; 4).
duration(2; 6; 8; 9).

% Single predicate to define the relation among diplomat, month, and duration.
assign(D, M, Dur) :- diplomat(D), month(M), duration(Dur).

% Ensure each diplomat is assigned exactly one month and one duration.
1 {assign(D, M, Dur) : month(M), duration(Dur)} 1 :- diplomat(D).

% Ensure each month is used exactly once.
:- month(M), {assign(D, M, Dur) : diplomat(D), duration(Dur)} != 1.

% Ensure each duration is used exactly once.
:- duration(Dur), {assign(D, M, Dur) : diplomat(D), month(M)} != 1.

% Clue 1: Howell will leave sometime after Fitzgerald.
:- assign("Howell", M1, _), assign("Fitzgerald", M2, _), M1 =< M2.

% Clue 2: Howell will leave sometime before the ambassador with the 6 day visit.
:- assign("Howell", M1, _), assign(D, M2, 6), M1 >= M2.

% Clue 3: Vasquez will leave 1 month before the ambassador with the 2 day visit.
:- assign("Vasquez", M1, _), assign(D, M2, 2), M2 != M1 + 1.

% Clue 4: The ambassador with the 8 day visit will leave 2 months before Vasquez.
:- assign(D, M1, 8), assign("Vasquez", M2, _), M2 != M1 + 2.

% Output the assignment.
#show assign/3.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
rockets("Beritron"; "Exatris"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Omnipax"; "Rubicorp"; "SpaceZen"; "Ubersplore").

% Define the launch relation: launches_in(R, M, C) means rocket R, developed by company C, launches in month M.
% Each rocket launches in exactly one month.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : months(M), companies(C)} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% Each company develops exactly one rocket.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), months(M)} = 1 :- companies(C).

% Each month has exactly one rocket launch.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), companies(C)} = 1 :- months(M).

% Constraint: Exatris launches exactly 1 month after Worul.
:- launches_in("Worul", M), launches_in("Exatris", M1), M1 != M + 1, months(M), months(M1).

% 2.1 The Foltron and the rocket that will launch in January are different.
:- launches_in("Foltron", 1, _), launches_in(R, 1, _), R != "Foltron".

% 2.2 The Foltron is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in("Foltron", M, "Ubersplore"); launches_in("Foltron", M, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- months(M).

% 2.3 The rocket that launches in January is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in(R, 1, "Ubersplore"); launches_in(R, 1, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% 3. The rocket developed by SpaceZen launches 2 months after the rocket developed by Ubersplore.
:- launches_in(R1, M1, "Ubersplore"), launches_in(R2, M2, "SpaceZen"), M2 != M1 + 2.

% 4. The rocket that launches in February is either the Worul or developed by Omnipax.
:- months(2), not ( launches_in("Worul", 2, _) ; launches_in(_, 2, "Omnipax") ).

% Additional constraints to ensure consistency and uniqueness:
% Worul cannot launch in month 4 (since Exatris must launch the next month).
:- launches_in("Worul", 4).

% Ubersplore cannot launch in month 3 or 4 (because SpaceZen must launch 2 months later).
:- launches_in(_, M, "Ubersplore"), M > 2.

% SpaceZen cannot launch in month 1 or 2 (because Ubersplore must launch 2 months earlier).
:- launches_in(_, M, "SpaceZen"), M < 3.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
rockets("Beritron"; "Exatris"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Omnipax"; "Rubicorp"; "SpaceZen"; "Ubersplore").

% Define the launch relation: launches_in(R, M, C) means rocket R, developed by company C, launches in month M.
% Each rocket launches in exactly one month.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : months(M), companies(C)} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% Each company develops exactly one rocket.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), months(M)} = 1 :- companies(C).

% Each month has exactly one rocket launch.
{launches_in(R, M, C) : rockets(R), companies(C)} = 1 :- months(M).

% Constraint: Exatris launches exactly 1 month after Worul.
:- launches_in("Worul", M), launches_in("Exatris", M1), M1 != M + 1, months(M), months(M1).

% 2.1 The Foltron and the rocket that will launch in January are different.
:- launches_in("Foltron", 1, _), launches_in(R, 1, _), R != "Foltron".

% 2.2 The Foltron is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in("Foltron", M, "Ubersplore"); launches_in("Foltron", M, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- months(M).

% 2.3 The rocket that launches in January is made by Ubersplore or Rubicorp.
{launches_in(R, 1, "Ubersplore"); launches_in(R, 1, "Rubicorp")} = 1 :- rockets(R).

% 3. The rocket developed by SpaceZen launches 2 months after the rocket developed by Ubersplore.
:- launches_in(R1, M1, "Ubersplore"), launches_in(R2, M2, "SpaceZen"), M2 != M1 + 2.

% 4. The rocket that launches in February is either the Worul or developed by Omnipax.
:- months(2), not ( launches_in("Worul", 2, _) ; launches_in(_, 2, "Omnipax") ).

% Additional constraints to ensure consistency and uniqueness:
% Worul cannot launch in month 4 (since Exatris must launch the next month).
:- launches_in("Worul", 4).

% Ubersplore cannot launch in month 3 or 4 (because SpaceZen must launch 2 months later).
:- launches_in(_, M, "Ubersplore"), M > 2.

% SpaceZen cannot launch in month 1 or 2 (because Ubersplore must launch 2 months earlier).
:- launches_in(_, M, "SpaceZen"), M < 3.
```
